Patch Set 1:

Shilong had a patch for this - https://review.whamcloud.com/39317 - that was a bit more complex...  Not sure if you've seen that one?

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 1:

1. All kernels back *quite* a ways - I just checked back to 2.6.32.

2. I have.  This just bails out to buffered I/O (which is what GPFS does), Shilong's patch does something very different - it tries to allow unaligned i/o *via the DIO path*.  It also doesn't work in the general case - I can get in to why elsewhere (it's just a lot to explain now).

Patch Set 1:

Meant to note: 2.6.32 is CentOS 6.  (Which you probably have burned in to your brain just like me, but just in case.)
Patch Set 2: Published edit on patch set 1.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

Converted a stray return to RETURN().

Still needs tests added before it can be considered for landing, but seems promising.

This turned out to be much harder than expected, so we're just going to do unaligned direct I/O support instead, see LU-13805

parents.subject: LU-14930 mdt: abort_recov_mdt shouldn't abort client recovery

commit.subject: LU-14969 llite: DIO fall back to buffered
commit.message:
LU-14969 llite: DIO fall back to buffered

Allow unaligned DIO by falling back to buffered I/O.
The kernel handles this automatically when we return 0 in
direct I/O.

We do not allow unaligned AIO because AIO is expecting DIO
behavior and it is extra work to make fallback work for
AIO.

Signed-off-by: Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>
